description:
## Bug

`pattern_check` treats a guarded clause as if its pattern alone covered the
input space, so the next clause is incorrectly marked unreachable AND its
binders are elided in lowering, surfacing as `error[lower/unbound]`:

```fz
fn lt_filter(p, [h | rest]) when h < p, do: [h | lt_filter(p, rest)]
fn lt_filter(p, [h | rest]),            do: lt_filter(p, rest)
```

Today: clause 2 warns "earlier clauses cover every input" (false — `when h < p`
doesn't cover h >= p), then `error[lower/unbound]: unbound: rest`.

## Fix

In `pattern_check`, a guarded clause does **not** contribute to the covered
pattern set for exhaustiveness/redundancy purposes. Standard ML/Erlang
semantics: guards are runtime predicates, not part of static pattern coverage.

Two analyses use this:
- "this clause is unreachable" → only fires when prior **unguarded** clauses cover.
- "fn clauses don't cover every input" → consider only unguarded clauses' patterns.

## Tests

- `lt_filter`-shaped fixture compiles + runs (will land in `fz-aiz.5` as quicksort).
- Unit test in `pattern_check`: clause 2 (unguarded) after clause 1 (guarded with same pattern) is **not** unreachable.
- Unit test: guarded-clause-only function still warns "doesn't cover every input."

## Independent of other children. Leaf.
title: fz-aiz.2: pattern exhaustiveness respects guards
